data.title: Monolithic vs. Microservices
data.description: Google employs a microservices architecture to enhance scalability, allowing each service to operate independently and communicate over APIs.
data.technical_spec: Microservices; APIs for communication; Independent deployments

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Google uses global load balancers to distribute incoming traffic efficiently and prevent overloading servers.
data.technical_spec: Global load balancing; Auto-scaling; Traffic distribution

data.title: Data Centers
data.description: Utilizes geographically distributed data centers to ensure high availability and redundancy.
data.technical_spec: Global data centers; Redundancy; High availability

data.title: Network Infrastructure
data.description: Robust network infrastructure with custom-built data cables and peering agreements.
data.technical_spec: Custom data cables; Peering; Network optimization

data.title: Containerization
data.description: Extensive use of containers (e.g., Kubernetes) for deploying applications to ensure consistency across environments.
